codeReview.summary: Lock-path relay pump shutdown is implemented correctly, but the unsaved-changes guard is incomplete: one route-change affordance in Settings bypasses the confirm dialog and can silently discard edits, so VAL-SETTINGS-029 is not fully met.

issues.description: The Replace Share CTA performs `onClose(); navigate('/replace-share')` directly instead of going through `guardNav(...)`. With an unsaved Profile Name draft or partial Change Password form, this route change drops in-memory edits without confirmation or auto-save, violating the feature's navigate-away requirement (VAL-SETTINGS-029 forbids silent loss on route change).

The deviation doc claims route-level navigation (including Replace Share) is covered because the parent `onClose()` path triggers the guard, but current code bypasses `guardNav` on the Replace Share button. The documentation is out of sync with implementation.

sharedStateObservations.observation: UI-validation guidance is inconsistent across shared state: AGENTS requires agent-browser for UI validation, while the ui-screen-worker skill permits some skips. This ambiguity can allow behavioral UI changes to ship without manual UI verification.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Mission AGENTS.md line 84 says "The `agent-browser` skill is mandatory for UI validation"; `.factory/skills/ui-screen-worker/SKILL.md` line 23 allows skips for "Pure copy-string / neutral-fallback / data-wiring fixes"; this worker reported `followedProcedure: false` and skipped agent-browser in the handoff for a new confirm-dialog interaction.

summary: Reviewed handoff, transcript skeleton, commit diff, and shared-state docs for m5-lock-and-unsaved-changes. Result: fail due to a blocking unsaved-changes gap on Replace Share route navigation, plus one non-blocking documentation mismatch.
